title: build_shielded_tx tests: all error paths
description:
Exhaustive error-path tests for build_shielded_tx. Every TxBuildError variant must have at least one test that:
(a) constructs the exact triggering condition
(b) asserts the specific variant is returned (not just 'is_err()')
(c) does not panic under any circumstance

Tests:

1. dust_amount_rejected
   - amount_zatoshi = DUST_THRESHOLD - 1 → TxBuildError::DustAmount { amount, threshold }
   - amount_zatoshi = 0 → also DustAmount
   - amount_zatoshi = DUST_THRESHOLD exactly → Ok (boundary)

2. insufficient_funds_exact
   - total_note_value = amount + fee - 1 (one zatoshi short) → TxBuildError::InsufficientFunds { have, need }
   - Verify have < need in the error

3. no_spendable_notes
   - Empty notes slice → TxBuildError::NoSpendableNotes

4. invalid_recipient_address
   - 'not_an_address' → TxBuildError::InvalidRecipient
   - Empty string → TxBuildError::InvalidRecipient
   - A mainnet address passed to a testnet builder (or vice versa) → TxBuildError::InvalidRecipient or UnsupportedAddressType

5. transparent_address_rejected
   - A valid t1... transparent address → TxBuildError::UnsupportedAddressType

6. anchor_mismatch
   - Two notes with witnesses at different block heights (different Merkle roots) → TxBuildError::AnchorMismatch

7. amount_overflow
   - amount_zatoshi = u64::MAX → TxBuildError::AmountOverflow (cannot add fee)

8. corrupt_witness_data
   - SpendableNote with witness_data = vec![0x00; 5] (too short) → TxBuildError::WitnessDeserialize

9. corrupt_note_plaintext
   - SpendableNote with note_g_d = wrong length → TxBuildError::NoteDeserialize

Defensive: each test must assert the exact enum variant, not just is_err(). Use matches!() macro for variant matching.
acceptance_criteria: each TxBuildError variant has at least one test; all tests assert exact variant; running under cargo test --workspace --release also passes (no debug-only behavior)
close_reason: error-path tests for build_shielded_tx implemented and passing (134 tests total)
